untranslatable_reason: Signal requires detection of 'central bank/government intervention announcement' and 'consensus sentiment percentile' which are external events/sentiment data not available in the allowed indicator set. The rule also requires 'trader conviction score' which is subjective and not quantifiable from price/technical data alone. Approximated entry conditions using RSI >75 (extreme bullish consensus proxy) and recent rally (returns >3%), but the core intervention detection and conviction scoring cannot be mechanically implemented.
translation_notes: Converted CONTEXT_DEPENDENT direction to SHORT (counter-trend positioning) based on 'sell the rally' principle. Used rsi_14 >75 as a proxy for extremely bullish sentiment (>75th percentile). Used returns >3% to approximate a 3-5% rally confirmation. Exit condition uses reversal logic (close > prev_close). The fundamental mechanics of detecting government intervention and measuring trader conviction require external data feeds or manual signal injection.
